rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES AIKINS VS. RPI COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike, Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 25, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant RPI Company¿s motion to strike or tax costs: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. The Court is prepared to deny defendant¿s motion to strike plaintiff¿s memorandum of costs in its entirety and grant in part and deny in part defendant¿s motion to tax. To the hearing defendant must bring a proposed order attached to which is a spreadsheet showing the costs claimed and defendant¿s objections to each, like defendant¿s Exhibit E that also includes a column for the Court to rule on each item. Plaintiff¿s opposition violated the page limits set forth in CRC 3.1113 and the Court refuses to consider anything beyond page 15. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
